body_txt: The Protagonist and friends have suffered the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune, taken arms against the sea of troubles, and prevailed . The Big Bad lies slain. The Cavalry has saved the day. The lost friends have been found . The Final Battle is won, the wedding prevented and we all have yelled yes ! But wait. The story is not over yet . Our protagonists have been so engrossed with this huge task, you see, they completely forgot about something, at best, loosely related to said task that only now becomes relevant. Deadly relevant. It can be the dead-man's switch the villain's put on their lair , or the Perpetual-Motion Monster that has been chasing them since act one (and only caught up now ). Or maybe they have taken care of everything they had been supposed to take care of, only for a breathless messenger to rush in with the news of a sudden Outside-Context Problem . The messenger, or the group's Only Sane Man , cuts the celebration short with a sober reminder. Don't celebrate just yet . This is mostly a comedy trope, though sometimes played seriously. Sometimes a disaster could be averted if someone had just remembered it. Compare Out of the Frying Pan and Hope Spot .
